title: BL-200 message-passing over LoRa — route an Event A->B->C, MASK-forced multi-hop
runner: hive; 3 tuxedo DFR1195 — A=480e900e (mac 50:26:98), B=2cab5f69 (mac b7:90:10), C=f91c8911 (mac b6:0a:a0); features nobt,loraroute,loratcxo,multitg; core's r2-sx1262 + r2-transport LoRaTransport data-plane; via my ssh to tuxedo serial
hardware: DFR1195 (FireBeetle 2 ESP32-S3) + SX1262; SPI3 SCK7/MISO5/MOSI6, NSS10, RST41, BUSY40, RXEN42, DIO1=4; TCXO DIO3 1.8V
config: wairoa_as923_nz bench: 916.8MHz/BW125/SF7(bench)/CR45/sync0x21/CRC/explicit/TCXO1.8V/tx_power-9dBm(bench). LoRa-carrier: 4B immediate-sender prepend; hive-MASK A={B} B={A,C} C={B}; ALOHA TX-jitter 0-300ms (loose-liveness de-collision)
firmware_commit: r2-hive (loraroute; this run = jitter iteration on top of 4042042)

expectations.observed: B ingests both A and C from HBs (nbrs=2); A and C each hear only B at the engine (A,C correctly MASK-drop the other). RSSI A<->C -33dBm, A/C<->B -48..-51dBm — strong links.

B logs directed_via from=2cab5f69 next_hop=f91c8911 (A->C, msg_id=2) and next_hop=480e900e (C->A replies, msg_id=32769/32770) — the §4.3.4 used-path-wins trail formed after the first delivery+reply. (msg_id=1 flooded fanout=1 before the trail existed.)

C delivers ONLY B-relayed copies; A's direct frames to C are MASK-dropped at C (LORA-RX sender=480e900e masked=true x26) and C's direct frames at A masked (sender=f91c8911 masked=true x10). A<->C never direct.
C DELIVERED msg_id=1 and msg_id=2 exactly once each (dlv=2); B logs DROP Duplicate x4 on its own relayed echoes. Per-(origin=A, msg_id) dedup (the fr_origin fix) holds multi-hop.
C fires RECEIPT_SIGNAL on each DELIVERED (LED bright ~400ms; heartbeat envelope suppressed under loraroute). LED-driver path exercised twice; Roy's visual confirmation recommended for the demo.
C emitted replies (reply_msg_id high-bit 32769/32770); each routed C->B->A and DELIVERED at A — full bidirectional multi-hop over LoRa.

reliability_finding: Per-message delivery rate ~2/19 over 120s at SF7 with the always-on tight-PCO heartbeat: half-duplex synchronized-fire collisions drop most frames (B's TX reached A/C only ~1/100s under lockstep; the ALOHA TX-jitter lifted that enough to prove the path, but full reliability needs Roy's refinement — the heartbeat as LOOSE jittered BACKGROUND path-maintenance, not the tight lub-dub PCO + lower HB rate). CORRECTNESS is proven; reliability tuning is the follow-up (and feeds TN-FR-4 sensor/router/receiver).
notes: FIRST message-passing metal result on the Wairoa radio (TN-FR-1). The PROVEN io_task r2-route + §4.3.4 TrailReinforcer run over the LoRa carrier via core's READY LoRaTransport::service() data-plane (continuous-RX + TX-pacing + §4.2/§4.3 airtime-gating). The 4B immediate-sender prepend is the LoRa analogue of ESP-NOW's L2 src MAC on a MAC-less broadcast medium (feeds the can_hear MASK + the §4.3.4 src_hive=relayer); the R2-WIRE origin (A) is carried inside the frame -> ForwardRequest.origin (the BL-200/M-ESPNOW-3 fix, was hardcoded 0). RxenRadio newtype keeps the RF-switch (GPIO42) in the thin per-platform layer. Boards were the released D1/D2/D3 (all TG-A); baseline (2-TG demo) restored after.
